lines.target: On the train, David reads his paper.
lines.base: En el tren, David lee el (su) periódico.

lines.target: He stands because the train is full.
lines.base: Está de pie porque el tren está lleno.

lines.target: People that travel to work every day are called "commuters".
lines.base: [A] la gente que viaja al trabajo todos los días se la llaman (son llamados) « commuters »,
lines.pronunciation: .. evii dei. kómiulérs. — 4... doh i tel yorni leiks... uok.. — 5... yok ikli, — 6... 7 --. teiks dze lift... forz floor. — 8 Si ce iS... liivs.., — 10 kualt niér. — 12 ... selrálir, skaunt-

lines.target: The journey takes twenty minutes and he has ten minutes to walk to the office.
lines.base: El viaje dura (toma) 20 minutos y tarda (tiene) diez minutos para llegar (caminar) hasta la oficina.

lines.target: He hasn't much time, so he walks quickly,
lines.base: No tiene mucho tiempo, por eso anda deprisa.

lines.target: He crosses the City and arrives at the office.
lines.base: Atraviesa la « City » y llega a la (su) oficina.

lines.target: He takes the lift to the fourth floor.
lines.base: Coge el ascensor hasta el cuarto piso.

lines.target: He goes to his desk and sits down. He is on time.
lines.base: Va a su mesa y se sienta. Llega a tiempo.

lines.target: His wife Joan washes the dishes and leaves the house at ten to nine.
lines.base: Su mujer Joan friega los platos y se va (deja la) casa a las nueve menos diez.

lines.target: Her office is quite near, so she always walks.
lines.base: Su oficina está muy cerca, por eso va siempre andando (siempre anda).

lines.target: It takes her eight minutes to arrive at her office.
lines.base: Tarda (ello le toma) 8 minutos en llegar a la (su) oficina.

lines.target: She is a secretary in an accountants firm.
lines.base: Es (una) secretaria en una empresa de contabilidad.

lines.target: At nine o'clock, both the Wilsons are working.
lines.base: A [las] nueve los dos Wilson están trabajando.

gap_candidates.target: On the train, David reads his paper.
He stands because the train is full. (1)
People that travel to work every day are called "commuters".
The journey takes twenty minutes and he has ten minutes to walk to the office.
He hasn't much time, so he walks quickly,
He crosses the City and arrives at the office.
He takes the lift to the fourth floor.
He goes to his desk and sits down. He is on time. (3) (4)
His wife Joan washes the dishes and leaves the house at ten to nine. (5) (6)
Her office is quite near, so she always walks.
It takes her eight minutes to arrive at her office.
She is a secretary in an accountants firm.
At nine o'clock, both the Wilsons are working.
